§6ME IO Port§f:

§6Production Path (network/blocks/io_port.json)§f:
Pattern "aaa / bcb / ded":
- §ba§f: §6Glass §f(any of the §6#appliedenergistics2:quartz_glass §foredict tag) — 3 (top row)
- §bb§f: §6ME Drive §f(appliedenergistics2:drive) — 1 (middle left)
- §cc§f: §6ME Glass Cable - Fluix §f(appliedenergistics2:part:16) — 1 (centre)
- §cd§f: §6Logic Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:22) — 1 (bottom middle)
- §ed§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 2 (bottom corners)
- Yields 1× ME IO Port

§6Function (BlockIOPort.java)§f:
- §bStorage multiplexer §f: combine up to §b9 §fME Drives in a 3×3 frame around the port
- §bNetwork I/O §f: items enter/leave the network through the port, distributed across the attached drives
- §bCell view §f: open the port GUI to see §6all cells §f in the attached drives in one place
- §bNo crafting function §f: an IO Port §cdoes not §f host §6autocrafting patterns §f — use an §6ME Pattern Terminal §f on an §6ME Interface §f for that

- §eTip§f: an IO Port without attached drives is just a §6fluix-cable-bound §f object; place at least §b1 §fDrive to make it useful
- §cWarning§f: drives inside an IO Port are §6isolated §f from the network until a §6Controller §f connects

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 24 items the ME IO Port is a component of, including:
- §6Spatial IO Port §f(appliedenergistics2:spatial_io_port)
- §6ME Drive §f(appliedenergistics2:drive)
- §6Crafting patterns §f that involve the IO Port

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: place the IO Port at the §6centre §f of a §6subnet §f; the port's GUI lists all cells in the attached drives, including cells partitioned to specific items
- §cWarning§f: the IO Port does §cnot §f allow removing the underlying ME Drive; §6break the IO Port §f first to retrieve the drive intact
- §cWarning§f: the IO Port uses §b1 channel §f from the controller; plan cabling accordingly
